phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 the numbers lean your way — point-click stun burst edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Slight edge at level 2: your point-click stun burst beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean your way — point-click stun burst edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your point-click stun burst beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — point-click stun burst edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your point-click stun burst on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Respect window: at two-plus items the wall-frost poke control beat your point-click stun burst cleanly. Trade only off Anivia's cooldowns and keep the wave where the lead can't cash out.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q last-hits refund mana on kill — farm with it for free and let the stun counter tick where he can see it.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W joins the stack engine: the cone clears and counts. A stocked stun at level 2 already makes him flinch.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E shield completes the loop: stack the passive on your own shield safely. Trade only with the stun loaded.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 your point-click stun burst beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Tibbers online — Flash-R-W-Q deletes most mids from full health. The lane is now a geometry problem about your Flash range.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Luden's/Stormsurge spike: the stun-burst math kills through Doran's defenses. Stock, walk forward, win.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Annie flips fights with one button: flash-Tibbers the clump, peel with the bear, and re-stock off the chaos.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q last-hits refund mana on kill — farm with it for free and let the stun counter tick where he can see it.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W joins the stack engine: the cone clears and counts. A stocked stun at level 2 already makes him flinch.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean your way — point-click stun burst edge out the reflect-barrier poke when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight edge through levels 4-5: your point-click stun burst beat Mel's reflect-barrier poke on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Tibbers online — Flash-R-W-Q deletes most mids from full health. The lane is now a geometry problem about your Flash range.

phases.why: Slight edge at two-plus items: your point-click stun burst beat Pantheon's point-click spear bullying on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q last-hits refund mana on kill — farm with it for free and let the stun counter tick where he can see it.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W joins the stack engine: the cone clears and counts. A stocked stun at level 2 already makes him flinch.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E shield completes the loop: stack the passive on your own shield safely. Trade only with the stun loaded.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Hold the four-stack glow and watch him orbit at max range — a stocked Annie controls the lane by standing there.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight edge at the level-6 breakpoint: your point-click stun burst beat Yasuo's windwall dash-weaving on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Respect window: on first item the windwall dash-weaving beat your point-click stun burst cleanly. Trade only off Yasuo's cooldowns and keep the wave where the lead can't cash out.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Annie flips fights with one button: flash-Tibbers the clump, peel with the bear, and re-stock off the chaos.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Tibbers online — Flash-R-W-Q deletes most mids from full health. The lane is now a geometry problem about your Flash range.
